task_completion.description: The Export dialog is opened in the Subsurface application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Export' or similar is visible over the main Subsurface window.

task_completion.description: HTML is selected as the export format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Export dialog, the 'HTML' option (or 'HTML web page') is selected from the list of available formats.

task_completion.description: The option to export all dives is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Export dialog, the option for 'All dives' (or similar wording) is selected, ensuring that the export is not limited to only selected dives.

task_completion.description: The export destination path is correctly specified.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog or path input field shows the destination directory as 'WebLog' inside 'Documents' and the filename as 'divelog.html'.
The agent clicks the 'Save' or 'Export' button to finalize the process, and the dialog closes. The resulting 'divelog.html' file may also be visible in a file manager or terminal at the correct path.

integrity.description: The agent used the Subsurface graphical interface to perform the export.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Subsurface application menus and export dialogs, rather than terminal commands to generate the file.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually create a fake HTML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent opening a text editor to write HTML code or using commands to create a dummy file named divelog.html.

integrity.description: The agent did not copy an existing HTML file to bypass the export process.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen navigating the Subsurface export flow rather than using a file manager or terminal to copy files from elsewhere.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is '/home/ga/Documents/WebLog/divelog.html'. The exported HTML file must contain dive data, including the location 'Sund Rock', and its size will be significantly larger than 10,000 bytes. The source data is from Subsurface's SampleDivesV2.ssrf.
